title: भगवद्गीता
subtitle: INTRODUCTION TO THE DIVINE SONG
description: The Bhagavad Gita is a 700-verse Hindu scripture set on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. It is a profound discourse on duty (dharma), devotion (bhakti), and selfless action (karma yoga), delivering the eternal essence of liberation.

cards.title: THE SETTING
cards.content: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, amidst the clash of two great armies, the eternal yoga of wisdom was revealed.

cards.title: THE TEACHING
cards.content: Krishna imparts the knowledge of Karma, Bhakti, and Jnana, guiding the soul from despondency to liberation.

cards.title: THE CORE
cards.content: You have a right to your actions, but never to their fruits. Absolute surrender to the divine is the ultimate secret.

chapters.name: Arjuna Vishada Yoga
chapters.summary: On the battlefield, Arjuna is overwhelmed by grief and moral confusion. He drops his bow, lamenting the futility of war against his own kinsmen.

chapters.name: Sankhya Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna teaches the immortality of the soul — the body perishes but the soul never dies. He introduces the ideal of a person with steady wisdom.

chapters.name: Karma Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna explains that action is inevitable and teaches 'nishkama karma' — performing one's duties without attachment to the fruits of action.

chapters.name: Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna reveals his divine nature and the doctrine of avatara. He explains how all actions can be transcended through transcendental knowledge.

chapters.name: Karma Sanyasa Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna reconciles action and renunciation. True renunciation is inner freedom from ego and desire, acting while remaining untouched like a lotus.

chapters.name: Dhyana Yoga
chapters.summary: Details the practice of meditation and mental discipline. The mind is the greatest friend or enemy; yoga leads to union with the divine.

chapters.name: Jnana Vijnana Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna reveals his nature as the ultimate reality pervading all creation. He distinguishes between material and higher spiritual natures.

chapters.name: Akshara Brahma Yoga
chapters.summary: Explains the concepts of Brahman and karma. Whatever one remembers at death determines the next birth; the path of light leads to liberation.

chapters.name: Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga
chapters.summary: The 'royal secret': Krishna pervades the universe yet transcends it. Pure devotion through simple offerings of love is accepted by the divine.

chapters.name: Vibhuti Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna describes his divine glories and manifestations in the world — he is the best among all categories, helping us see the divine in all.

chapters.name: Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga
chapters.summary: Arjuna receives divine vision and sees Krishna's awe-inspiring cosmic form (Vishwarupa), embodying the entire universe and the power of Time.

chapters.name: Bhakti Yoga
chapters.summary: The path of personal devotion is declared superior. Krishna describes the qualities of a perfect devotee: friendly, compassionate, and firm in faith.

chapters.name: Kshetra Kshetragna Vibhaga Yoga
chapters.summary: Distinguishes between the physical body ('the field') and the conscious self ('the knower'). Understanding this leads to liberation.

chapters.name: Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga
chapters.summary: Explains the three modes of nature: Sattva (goodness), Rajas (passion), and Tamas (ignorance). Transcending these leads to immortality.

chapters.name: Purushottama Yoga
chapters.summary: The metaphor of the inverted tree of samsara. Krishna reveals himself as the Supreme Person (Purushottama) beyond the perishable and imperishable.

chapters.name: Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga
chapters.summary: Contrasts divine qualities (fearlessness, purity) with demoniac ones (arrogance, pride). Lust, anger, and greed are the three gates to hell.

chapters.name: Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga
chapters.summary: Classifies faith, food, and sacrifice according to the three gunas. 'Om Tat Sat' is the foundation of all sacred and spiritual actions.

chapters.name: Moksha Sanyasa Yoga
chapters.summary: Synthesis of all teachings. Krishna declares bhakti as the ultimate path: abandon all dharmas and surrender to him for final liberation.
